source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
About Wayflyer

Today's small businesses need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer.

Our technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. To date, we've deployed over $6bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan.

You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world. We have offices in Dublin, London, New York, Charlotte, Berlin and Sydney.



The challenge

We've deployed over $6bn to small businesses that traditional banks were too slow, too rigid, or too disinterested to back. Now we're growing fast, and we need a world-class sales team to continue the momentum, finding the founders who need us before they know we exist.



What you'll actually do

You'll be the first voice a lot of founders ever hear from Wayflyer. That matters. Your job is to cut through the noise - smart outreach, real conversations, and get the right businesses in front of our Account Executives. You'll qualify funding needs, build genuine rapport with founders, and figure out pretty quickly that the best reps here aren't order-takers. They're curious, they hustle, and they care about getting it right.



What this role could turn into

We'll be honest -this isn't a role we're hiring you to stay in forever. It's a launchpad. Most BDRs move into Account Executive or Customer Success roles within 12–18 months, and we plan for it actively through our Revenue Academy - a structured programme that builds the skills, deal exposure, and commercial fluency you need before you ever step into your next seat. Your manager isn't just hitting you with pipeline reviews; they're invested in where you want to go and what it takes to get there.

We're building careers here, not just filling headcount. The people who thrive with us are the ones who are hungry to grow - and we take it as our responsibility to make sure the path is there when they are.



Who thrives here

You've worked in a target-driven environment before and you didn't just survive it, you wanted more. You're the kind of person who reads a rejection email and immediately thinks about what to try differently. You spot problems outside your lane and you can't help yourself. You'll probably end up doing things that aren't in this job description. That's not a warning, it's the point.



We hire for range

We care more about trajectory than tenure. Relevant sales experience is important, but what we're really looking for beyond that is someone who's hungry, commercially sharp, and genuinely excited by the challenge. Fluent English is a must. Startup experience is a bonus, not a requirement.



Location and working policy

📍Dublin HQ, hybrid (3-4 days per week in the office)



The good stuff

25 days off, plus public holidays. Private healthcare, life insurance, and a pension. Equity - because you should own a piece of what you're building. Generous parental leave for primary and secondary caregivers. 60 days a year to work abroad from wherever you want.



By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Wayflyer Limited will process your personal data for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for the role. Such processing is based on the need to take steps prior to entering into a potential employment agreement. To learn more about how we handle your personal data, you can contact our privacy team at privacy@wayflyer.com or review our privacy notice at https://wayflyer.com/privacy-notice.



#LI-JW1

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About Wayflyer

Today's small businesses need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer.

Our technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. To date, we've deployed over $6bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan.

You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world. We have offices in Dublin, London, New York, Charlotte, Berlin and Sydney.



The challenge

There are thousands of businesses in North America growing fast and hitting the same wall - they need capital, and traditional lenders are too slow, too rigid, or simply not interested. We've already deployed over $6bn to growing businesses like these. The opportunity in front of us is significant, and we need people who can capitalise on it.



What you'll actually do

You'll own the full sales cycle from sourcing your own leads to closing deals with some of the most exciting consumer brands on the market. You'll run in-depth discovery calls using MEDDPICC, work closely with our underwriting team to move fast without cutting corners, and negotiate deals that genuinely work for both sides. You'll have a dedicated BDR alongside you, but you won't wait around for a full pipeline to land in your lap. The best AEs here source relentlessly, close confidently, and treat their customers like business partners, not transactions. Top performers are hitting ~200% of target. That's not a boast. It's a signal of what’s possible.



What this role could turn into

Account Executives at Wayflyer tend to grow in one of two directions - first on the ground in one of our emerging verticals, or into team leadership as we scale. We're building out North America seriously, which means the people who perform here will have real options. We'd rather promote from within than hire from outside, and our track record reflects that.



Who thrives here
You've worked a full sales cycle before, and you didn't just hit your number; you understood exactly why you hit it and what to do differently when you didn't. You source your own pipeline without being asked, and you don't wait for perfect conditions to start. You're energised by ambiguity, not paralysed by it. You push deals forward when others would wait for a signal. When a sceptical founder pushes back, you lean in. You've been told "no" and come back with a better question. You care about e-commerce and small business growth, not as a line on a cover letter, but as a genuine reason you want this job.

We hire for range
Full sales cycle experience in SaaS or FinTech is essential, ideally with time spent as an outbound BDR/SDR, you know what it takes to build a pipeline from scratch, not just work it. A consistent track record of exceeding targets matters more than tenure. Resilience shows up in your history: you've had a bad quarter, figured out why, and bounced back stronger. MEDDPICC familiarity is a plus. Startup or scale-up experience helps; the pace here is real, the playbook is still being written, and we need people who see that as an opportunity, not a risk.



Location and working policy

📍Charlotte, hybrid (3-4 days per week in the office)



The good stuff

25 days off, plus public holidays. Private healthcare, life insurance, and 401k. Equity - because you should own a piece of what you're building. Generous parental leave for primary and secondary caregivers. 60 days a year to work abroad from wherever you want.




By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Wayflyer Limited will process your personal data for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for the role. Such processing is based on the need to take steps prior to entering into a potential employment agreement. To learn more about how we handle your personal data, you can

source_jobs.title: Credit Analyst, New Verticals
source_jobs.description:
About Wayflyer

Today's small businesses need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer.

Our technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. To date, we've deployed over $6bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan.

You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world. We have offices in Dublin, London, New York, Charlotte, Berlin and Sydney.



The challenge

We move fast. We've deployed over $6bn to small businesses since 2020, and the pace isn't slowing down. As our portfolio grows, so does the complexity - more customers, more data, more decisions to get right. We need someone who can look at a business, understand what's actually going on underneath the numbers, and help us fund the right ones.



What you'll actually do

 - You'll dig into the financial and operational data of new and existing customers, figuring out what makes their business tick, where the risks are, and whether Wayflyer is the right partner for them right now.

 - You'll join calls with customers, ask the questions that matter, and feed your findings back to the team.

 - You'll work closely with sales and operations to understand how deals are won and managed, and you'll have a hand in building the processes and products that shape how we underwrite.

 - This isn't a back-office role. You'll be making real decisions, at pace.



What this role could turn into

Analysts here don't stay analysts for long if they don't want to. The natural paths are into senior risk roles, portfolio management, or broader data analytics, depending on where your interests take you. We'll actively help you figure that out.



Who thrives here

You're comfortable sitting with ambiguity and still making a call. You can read a set of management accounts and know which number to pull on. You ask questions in client meetings that other people didn't think to ask. You care about getting it right, not just getting it done, and you're commercially sharp enough to know the difference between a risk worth taking and one that isn't.



We hire for range

1-2 years of relevant experience helps, as does a strong academic record in Business, Finance, Economics, Engineering, Law, or Computer Science. You'll need to be comfortable with financial reporting and confident presenting to senior stakeholders. Excel or SQL experience is a plus.A professional qualification (ACA, ACCA, CFA, CIMA) or working towards one is nice to have, but not a dealbreaker. What we care about most is how you think.



Location and Working Policy

📍Dublin or London, hybrid (3-4 days per week in the office)



The good stuff

25 days off, plus public holidays. Private healthcare, life insurance, and critical illness cover. Equity - because you should own a piece of what you're building. Generous parental and adoptive leave for primary and secondary caregivers. And 60 days a year to work abroad from wherever you want.



By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Wayflyer Limited will process your personal data for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for the role. Such processing is based on the need to take steps prior to entering into a potential employment agreement. To learn more about how we handle your personal data, you can contact our privacy team at privacy@wayflyer.com or review our privacy notice at https://wayflyer.com/privacy-notice.



#LI-CH1
#LI-Hybrid

source_jobs.title: Backend Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Wayflyer

Today's small businesses need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer.

Our technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. To date, we've deployed over $6bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan.

You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world. We have offices in Dublin, London, New York, Charlotte, Berlin and Sydney.



The challenge

We assess businesses in minutes and send funds in as little as 24 hours. The systems that make that possible need to be fast, reliable, and built to scale as we grow into new markets.



What you'll actually do

 - Design and develop scalable backend services using Python and Django, focusing on our core financial products

 - Build and maintain RESTful APIs that power our customer-facing applications and internal tools

 - Implement robust data models and database schemas to support our financial services infrastructure

 - Write clean, testable code with comprehensive unit and integration tests

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design and implement new features and improvements

 - Optimize application performance and ensure high availability of our services

 - Participate in code reviews and contribute to technical documentation

 - Monitor and maintain production systems, ensuring reliability and security



Who thrives here

You should be comfortable making and defending technical decisions, while remaining open to feedback and alternative approaches. You'll need to balance technical excellence with business needs and help guide the team toward pragmatic solutions.

You excel at stakeholder management and can effectively communicate technical concepts to non-technical audiences. You're proactive in identifying and addressing potential issues before they become problems.

We look for candidates with most or all of the below traits that can point to real experience applying them.

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field, or equivalent practical experience

 - 5+ years of professional experience in backend development with Python

 - Strong proficiency in Django, or other Python API frameworks (e.g. FastAPI, Flask)

 - Experience with SQL databases (preferably PostgreSQL) and ORM frameworks

 - Knowledge of API design principles and microservices architecture

 - Experience with AWS or similar cloud platforms

 - Solid understanding of version control with Git

 - Experience with testing frameworks (e.g., pytest, unittest)

 - Understanding of security best practices and data protection

 - Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail

 - Excellent communication abilities and proven track record of successful team collaboration

Given what we do, experience in financial services, fintech and business-to-business organisations is particularly interesting to us.



We hire for range

What you're like as a person and how you treat people is a big deal for us. People love working with you. You're helpful and generous with your time and knowledge. People love helping you back. People know where they stand with you.

You're always looking to learn and grow, on a personal and professional level. And you're always looking to help others learn and grow by sharing your own knowledge and experience.

You know why diversity, equality, inclusion and belonging matters, and speak up when we can do better.



What this role could turn into

Engineers here tend to grow in the direction they're most interested in - whether that's deepening technical specialism, moving into architecture, or taking on more cross-functional ownership as we scale.



Location and working policy

📍Fully remote, Europe.



The good stuff

25 days off, plus public hol

source_jobs.title: Frontend Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Wayflyer

Today's small businesses need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer.

Our technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. To date, we've deployed over $6bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan.

You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world. We have offices in Dublin, London, New York, Charlotte, Berlin and Sydney.



The challenge

When a founder logs into Wayflyer, what they see, and how easily they can make sense of it, directly affects whether they trust us with their business. We're building financial products that need to feel effortless.



What you'll actually do

 - Provide technical leadership in architectural decisions and champion our existing frontend best practices, advocating for our standards and helping push them forward across the team

 - Lead the design and implementation of complex frontend features using TypeScript and React, ensuring high-quality, reusable components that scale across our financial products

 - Partner with product and design teams to shape product outcomes, defining intuitive experiences that help e-commerce brands access and manage their funding. This goes beyond delivering UI; every engineer here plays a meaningful role in what we build and how it serves our customers

 - Own end-to-end delivery: shape solutions with product/design, build and test, ship to production and monitor/iterate using our observability stack.

 - Drive improvements in performance, accessibility, and user experience across our platform

 - Mentor engineers across the team and conduct thorough code reviews to maintain high quality standards

 - Work closely alongside our backend engineers, contributing across the stack when needed. We actively encourage curiosity and flexibility about where you can add value

 - Lead technical discussions and contribute to the team's technical strategy

 - Identify and proactively address technical debt and system scalability challenges



Who thrives here

You should be comfortable making and defending technical decisions, while remaining open to feedback and alternative approaches. You'll need to balance technical excellence with business needs and help guide the team toward pragmatic solutions.

You excel at stakeholder management and can effectively communicate technical concepts to non-technical audiences. You're proactive in identifying and addressing potential issues before they become problems. We look for candidates with most or all of the below traits that can point to real experience applying them.

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field, or equivalent practical experience

 - 5+ years of experience with deep expertise in frontend development

 - Advanced proficiency in TypeScript and extensive experience with React and modern frontend architectures

 - Expert knowledge of HTML5, CSS3 and JavaScript fundamentals with a focus on performance optimisation

 - Strong experience designing and implementing RESTful APIs and managing complex state, including hands-on experience with TanStack Query

 - Experience contributing to and working with design systems

 - Familiarity with backend technologies (Python, Django) and a willingness to contribute across the stack. This isn’t a hard requirement, but that flexibility matters to us.

 - Experience with CI/CD pipelines (especially GitHub Actions) and maintaining high-quality testing practices using tools like Storybook/Chromatic, Playwright, and Vitest

 - Proven track record of leading technical initiatives and mentoring engineers

 - Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to break down complex problems

 - Excellent communication abilities and experience managing stakeh

source_jobs.title: Senior Credit Risk Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
About Wayflyer

Today's small businesses need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer.

Our technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. To date, we've deployed over $6bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan.

You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world. We have offices in Dublin, London, New York, Charlotte, Berlin and Sydney.



The challenge

Wayflyer has deployed over $6bn in funding and we're not slowing down. Every credit decision we make is a bet on a business's real trajectory, and the models behind those bets need to get sharper and faster than they were yesterday. We're building the next generation of commercial credit, with AI running through every layer of the stack.



What you'll actually do

You'll own the full lifecycle of our credit risk models. We treat Machine Learning as a branch of software engineering here, so you'll write production-grade code, not just notebooks.

 - Lead the end-to-end build of credit risk models, treating ML as software engineering so everything you ship is fast, reproducible and robust.

 - Design modelling frameworks for decisioning, pricing and fraud that move company-wide P&L, not just a dashboard metric.

 - Build credit policies and lending strategies that expand our addressable market safely, pushing conversion and profitability up together.

Scientific integrity is non-negotiable. Model improvements need to be statistically significant, with no data leakage and no bias hiding in the numbers. You'll turn technical findings into business trade-offs leadership can act on, mapping ROC AUC straight to revenue and loss, and you'll set the bar for code quality: reviewing, mentoring, and contributing to the libraries the whole team relies on.

You'll stand out if you've worked on highly automated lending, price sensitivity modelling, or Customer Lifetime Value.



What this role could turn into

Senior Data Scientists here tend to grow into Staff or leadership roles with broader influence over risk strategy and team direction. We're building a quantitative risk function that genuinely drives P&L. The people who shape it early will have significant influence over where it goes.



Who thrives here

 - Gets restless when a model is "good enough" but not great

 - 4+ years building and maintaining production-grade ML systems

 - Cares as much about code quality as the statistics behind it

 - Strong grip on predictive modeling and credit risk concepts (IV, ROC AUC, SHAP, PD, EAD, LGD, EL)

 - As comfortable in a modern monorepo (Python, SQL, Snowflake, dbt, and the rest of the stack) as a software engineer would be

 - Would rather walk a commercial stakeholder through a ROC AUC curve and make them care about the revenue impact than hide behind jargon

 - Uses AI as an accelerant, not a crutch

 - Comfortable owning outcomes, not just tickets



We hire for range

You'll need real depth on credit risk concepts (PD, EAD, LGD), a solid grasp of modelling techniques like IV and SHAP, and comfort working across Snowflake, dbt, ZenML, Dagster and Weights & Biases. Experience in automated lending, price sensitivity modelling or CLV is a strong plus.



Location and working policy

📍Dublin HQ or London, hybrid.



The good stuff

25 days off, plus public holidays. Private healthcare, life insurance, and a pension. Equity, because you should own a piece of what you're building. Generous parental leave for primary and secondary caregivers. 60 days a year to work abroad from wherever you want.





By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Wayflyer Limited will process your personal data for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for the role. Such processing is based on the need

We'll be honest -this isn't a role we're hiring you to stay in forever. It's a launchpad. Most BDRs move into Account Executive or Customer Success roles within 12–18 months, and we plan for it actively through our Revenue Academy - a structured programme that builds the skills, deal exposure, and commercial fluency you need before you ever step into your next seat. Your manager isn't just hitting you with pipeline reviews; they're invested in where you want to go and what it takes to get there.

We're building careers here, not just filling headcount. The people who thrive with us are the ones who are hungry to grow - and we take it as our responsibility to make sure the path is there when they are.

 

Who thrives here

You've worked in a target-driven environment before and you didn't just survive it, you wanted more. You're the kind of person who reads a rejection email and immediately thinks about what to try differently. You spot problems outside your lane and you can't help yourself. You'll probably end up doing things that aren't in this job description. That's not a warning, it's the point.

 

We hire for range

We care more about trajectory than tenure. Relevant sales experience is important, but what we're really looking for beyond that is someone who's hungry, commercially sharp, and genuinely excited by the challenge. Fluent English & German is a must. Startup experience is a bonus, not a requirement.

 

Location and working policy

📍 Berlin, hybrid (3-4 days per week in the office)

 

The good stuff

25 days off, plus public holidays. Private healthcare, life insurance, and a pension. Equity - because you should own a piece of what you're building. Generous parental leave for primary and secondary caregivers. 60 days a year to work abroad from wherever you want.

 

By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Wayflyer Limited will process your personal data for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for the role. Such processing is based on the need to take steps prior to entering into a potential employment agreement. To learn more about how we handle your personal data, you can contact our privacy team at privacy@wayflyer.com or review our privacy notice at https://wayflyer.com/privacy-notice.

 

#LI-HM1

source_jobs.title: Senior Credit Analyst, New Verticals
source_jobs.description:
About Wayflyer

Today's small businesses need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer.

Our technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. To date, we've deployed over $6bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan.

You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world. We have offices in Dublin, London, New York, Charlotte, Berlin and Sydney.



The challenge

We move fast. We've deployed over $6bn to small businesses since 2020, and the pace isn't slowing down. As our portfolio grows, so does the complexity - more customers, more data, more decisions to get right. We need someone who can look at a business, understand what's actually going on underneath the numbers, and help us fund the right ones.



What you'll actually do

 - You'll dig into the financial and operational data of new and existing customers, figuring out what makes their business tick, where the risks are, and whether Wayflyer is the right partner for them right now.

 - You'll join calls with customers, ask the questions that matter, and feed your findings back to the team.

 - You'll work closely with sales and operations to understand how deals are won and managed, and you'll have a hand in building the processes and products that shape how we underwrite.

 - This isn't a back-office role. You'll be making real decisions, at pace.



What this role could turn into

Analysts here don't stay analysts for long if they don't want to. The natural paths are into senior risk roles, portfolio management, or broader data analytics, depending on where your interests take you. We'll actively help you figure that out.



Who thrives here

You're comfortable sitting with ambiguity and still making a call. You can read a set of management accounts and know which number to pull on. You ask questions in client meetings that other people didn't think to ask. You care about getting it right, not just getting it done, and you're commercially sharp enough to know the difference between a risk worth taking and one that isn't.



We hire for range

1-2 years of relevant experience helps, as does a strong academic record in Business, Finance, Economics, Engineering, Law, or Computer Science. You'll need to be comfortable with financial reporting and confident presenting to senior stakeholders. Excel or SQL experience is a plus. A professional qualification (ACA, ACCA, CFA, CIMA) or working towards one is nice to have, but not a dealbreaker. What we care about most is how you think.



Location and Working Policy

📍Charlotte, hybrid (3-4 days per week in the office)



The good stuff

25 days off, plus public holidays. Private healthcare, life insurance, and critical illness cover. Equity - because you should own a piece of what you're building. Generous parental and adoptive leave for primary and secondary caregivers. And 60 days a year to work abroad from wherever you want.



By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Wayflyer Limited will process your personal data for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for the role. Such processing is based on the need to take steps prior to entering into a potential employment agreement. To learn more about how we handle your personal data, you can contact our privacy team at privacy@wayflyer.com or review our privacy notice at https://wayflyer.com/privacy-notice.

source_jobs.title: Senior / Staff Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
About Wayflyer

Today's small businesses need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer.

Our technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. To date, we've deployed over $6bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan.

You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world. We have offices in Dublin, London, New York, Charlotte, Berlin and Sydney.



The Challenge

Wayflyer moves fast, and the product has to keep up. We're building financial tools that businesses actually rely on - complex workflows, dense data, high stakes - and we need a designer who can make all of that feel effortless. That's a hard problem. It's also a genuinely exciting one.

We're hiring at Senior+ or Staff level depending on where you are, what you've built, and how far your influence reaches.



What You'll Actually Do

 - Own complex product problems from discovery through delivery, helping define what we build, why it matters, and how success is measured.

 - Design end-to-end journeys, workflows, interaction models, and high-fidelity experiences across web and mobile products.

 - Prototype concepts early and often to explore ideas, align stakeholders, and accelerate learning.

 - Translate complex financial concepts, workflows, and data into intuitive and scalable user experiences.

 - Contribute to and evolve our design system, balancing consistency with thoughtful innovation.

 - Collaborate closely with engineers throughout implementation to ensure quality and thoughtful execution.

 - Challenge assumptions and help shape product decisions with a long-term platform mindset.

 - Participate in design critiques and contribute to a culture of feedback, learning, and continuous improvement.

 - For Staff-level candidates, help elevate design quality across teams through influence, mentorship, and strategic design leadership.



Who Thrives Here

 - You don’t wait for perfect requirements and enjoy helping define the problem before designing the solution.

 - You thrive in ambiguity and enjoy creating clarity where none exists.

 - You have strong product instincts and can balance customer needs, business goals, and technical realities.

 - You care deeply about design craft and believe details matter.

 - You know that trust is built through hundreds of small interactions, especially in financial products.

 - You believe simplification is one of the hardest and most valuable design skills.

 - You are comfortable challenging assumptions, advocating for customers, and raising the quality bar when it matters.

 - You enjoy collaborating across disciplines and influencing decisions through strong thinking and communication.

 - You use AI as part of your workflow to learn, explore, prototype, and improve the quality of your work.

 - You want to work in a fast-moving environment where initiative, curiosity, and impact are valued.



How You Think and Work

 - You have experience designing digital products, ideally within fintech, banking, lending, payments, or similarly complex domains.

 - You have a portfolio that demonstrates strong product thinking, interaction design excellence, and exceptional visual craft.

 - You connect customer needs, business objectives, and technical constraints to create meaningful product outcomes.

 - You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and independently driving work forward.

 - You know how to simplify complexity without oversimplifying the problem.

 - You use evidence, and data to inform decisions while maintaining strong product intuition.

 - You communicate clearly, tell compelling stories, and can influence decisions across different audiences.

 - You care about outcomes as much as outputs and understand how design contri

source_jobs.title: Finance Counsel
source_jobs.description:
About Wayflyer

Today's small businesses need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer.

Our technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. To date, we've deployed over $6bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan.

You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world. We have offices in Dublin, London, New York, Charlotte, Berlin and Sydney.



The challenge

Wayflyer moves fast and the legal infrastructure that supports how we fund ourselves and serve our merchants has to move with it. We need a finance lawyer who can hold the complexity of our debt facilities and lending products in one hand, and the needs of our commercial teams in the other. This isn't a role for someone who wants to sit behind a wall of disclaimers. It's for someone who wants to be in the room where decisions get made.



What you'll actually do

 - You'll be the primary legal voice on everything touching our external funding, implementing and maintaining debt facilities, working through intercreditor arrangements, and making sure our capital markets activity is properly supported end to end.

 - You'll work alongside our business teams on our core lending and merchant cash advance products, fielding queries from across our geographies and making sure the answers are actually useful to the people asking them.

 - You'll own our contractual templates and playbook, keeping them current, making them sharper, and rebuilding them when the business has grown past what they were designed for.

 - When we're launching new products or enhancing existing ones, you'll be the person stress-testing the legal side before we go live. And you'll manage local counsel across jurisdictions, which means knowing when to delegate, when to push back, and when to roll up your sleeves.



What this role could turn into

Finance lawyers at Wayflyer don't stay narrowly defined for long. This role has a natural path toward leading the legal function for a specific product vertical, or stepping up into a broader General Counsel track as we scale. There's also a real opportunity to shape how we use AI and legal tech across the team, if you're genuinely curious about that space, you'll have room to lead on it.



Who thrives here

You're the kind of lawyer who gives advice people can actually act on. You translate complexity without losing the nuance that matters, and you know the difference between managing risk and just saying no. You don't wait to be asked, if you spot a gap in a template or a problem in a process, you fix it. You're comfortable with ambiguity and pace, and you've learned that the best legal work often happens outside the strict boundaries of the brief you were given.



We hire for range

You're a qualified solicitor with around 4-6 years' PQE in banking and finance, whether from a leading firm or a comparable in-house environment. You have solid experience across facility documentation, security structuring, and cross-border financing. What matters more than where you've been, though, is how you work: commercially minded, transactionally confident, and genuinely interested in what happens when legal meets product.



Location and working policy

📍This role is based in our Dublin or London office, with a hybrid working policy.



The good stuff

25 days off, plus public holidays. Private healthcare, life insurance, and a pension. Equity - because you should own a piece of what you're building. Generous parental leave for primary and secondary caregivers. 60 days a year to work abroad from wherever you want.



By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Wayflyer Limited will process your personal data for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for the role. Such

notes: Wayflyer is a fintech company with a strong focus on sales and business development. While they have some tech roles, the majority of their job postings are in sales and business development.
